preamble:
WE, THE PEOPLE OF INDIA, having solemnly resolved to constitute India into a SOVEREIGN SOCIALIST SECULAR DEMOCRATIC REPUBLIC and to secure to all its citizens: JUSTICE, social, economic and political; LIBERTY of thought, expression, belief, faith and worship;
EQUALITY of status and of opportunity; and to promote among them all FRATERNITY assuring the dignity of the individual and the unity and integrity of the Nation; IN OUR CONSTITUENT ASSEMBLY this twenty-sixth day of November, 1949, do HEREBY ADOPT, ENACT AND GIVE TO OURSELVES THIS CONSTITUTION

articles.title: Name and territory of the Union
articles.simple_explanation: Defines India (Bharat) as a Union of States and specifies its territories.
key_points: India is a Union, not a federation of independent units.
Includes States, Union Territories, and any acquired areas.
articles.example: The creation of a new state like Telangana falls under understanding India's territory.
articles.do: Respect the integrity of the Union and its diverse states.
articles.dont: Assume states can separate or secede from India on their own.

articles.title: Equality before law
articles.simple_explanation: Guarantees that every person is equal before the law and entitled to equal protection of the laws.
key_points: No one is above the law.
Law applies equally to rich and poor, powerful and weak.
Allows reasonable classification for different groups (like children).
articles.example: Both a politician and a citizen follow the same legal procedures for a crime.
articles.do: Treat all individuals equally regardless of status.
articles.dont: Discriminate or claim special legal immunity.

articles.simple_explanation: Protects the freedom of speech, expression, assembly, association, movement, and residence.

articles.example: You can peacefully protest government policy or start a business.

articles.dont: Use speech to incite violence or disturb public order.

articles.title: Protection of life and personal liberty
articles.simple_explanation: Protects the right to life and personal liberty, which can only be taken away by proper legal procedure.
key_points: The most vital fundamental right.
Includes right to privacy, right to clean environment, and dignity.
Cannot be suspended even during Emergency.
articles.example: The government cannot arrest a person without showing valid legal reason.
articles.do: Safeguard personal space and dignity of others.
articles.dont: Deprive anyone of their liberty without following due process.

key_points: Includes respecting the National Flag and Anthem.
Duty to protect environment and develop scientific temper.

articles.example: Standing respectfully during the National Anthem or planting trees.
articles.do: Fulfill your citizenship duties for national progress.
